channel.title: Nikos S. Kostagiolas on Nikos S. Kostagiolas
channel.description: Recent content in Nikos S. Kostagiolas on Nikos S. Kostagiolas

item.title: LinkGeoML
item.description: This is the project I am assigned to at the Athena Center of Research and Innovation and involves the development of data analysis pipelines for geospatial and textual data.

item.title: Exploring different sequence representations and classification methods for the prediction of nucleosome positioning
item.description: [//]: # More detail can easily be written here using Markdown and $\rm \LaTeX$ math code.

item.title: A new inspiring theory about learning: capsules
item.description:
After viewing the discussion between G. Hinton and A. Ng at the end of the first week’s lesson of the Coursera Deep Learning Specialization I became truly excited about the ideas behind capsules, a new deep learning architecture proposed by Prof. G. Hinton.

Apparently, a capsule is a modality-specific part of a neural network which is used to represent a signle instance of a feature and all its different properties, this allowing the neural network to partition its representations among its different capsules (i.e. subsets of neurons) and, this, generalize better. Each capsule specializes in its own modality and gets enabled if and only if a particular feature is present at the input data.

Hence, a neural net based on the capsule architecture will not require a whole layer to be specialized in just one aspect of the data it is provided with, but instead it will involve little groups of neurons (i.e. capsules) to form their own low-level representations and then, by combining knowledge of two or more groups, it will decide about how higher representations will be formed. So, for detecting higher-level features at the top capsules, it is essential for the lower-level capsules leading to these capsules above to form the criteria for this particular detection.

Suppose, for example, that we have a pair of capsules specializing at detecting the presence of a mouth and that of a nose in a picture, respectively. Then, each one of these capsules needs to vote for what the parameters should be for a face, a voting which would conclude to an agreement should the spatial relationship between the mouth and the nose proves right.

Therefore, when two or more capsules of the same level come to an agreement about a capsule of the next level, he decision will most probably be true, due to the heavily increasing error rate as the dimensions are getting higher and higher. This is called routing by agreement. Prof. Hinton summarized the talk by stating his firm belief that this learning structure could possibly provide more sample-efficient learning, when limited training data is available.

item.title: Multi-modal Signal Analysis for Human Activity Recognition using Deep Architectures.
item.description: This research project examines the application of various Deep Learning architectures at Human Frailty Recognition (HAR) using multi-sensor data gathered from three different health centers in France, Greece and Cyprus.

item.title: Example Talk
item.description: Embed your slides or video here using shortcodes. Further details can easily be added using Markdown and $\rm \LaTeX$ math code.

item.title: Soundscape Quality Estimation in Urban Spaces
item.description: In this project we aim to train a global soundscape quality estimation system using a Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) architecture. The experiments conducted focus on multi-label classification of urban recordings usign a human-annotated dataset.

item.title: Genomic Text Mining for Nucleosome Positioning Pattern Recognition using N-gram Graphs
item.description: This project has formed the basis around which my B.Sc. thesis is structured. It involves the comparison between several Machine Learning representations and classification algorithms on their ability to produce useful intuitions on how the underlying genomic sequence influences nucleosome positioning and, consequently, forms regulational constraints on gene expression.

item.title: Energy Trading & Risk Management
item.description: I was involved with this project during my internship at IBM Hellas the purpose of which was to develop ETL dataflows and data analysis pipelines for our client, the Greek Department of Energy Management.

item.title: SOLVENCY II
item.description: I was involved with this project during my internship at IBM Hellas the purpose of which was to develop ETL dataflows for our client, “ETHNIKI” Hellenic General Insurance Company Group. SOLVENCY II was a crucial project the completion of which resulted in a report which included key figure and information of the Group’s business and performance, system of Governance, risk profile, valuation for solvency purposes, capital management and its future prospects.
